subject: Physics
title: How We Measured Light's Speed
description: Learn How We Measured Light's Speed in one minute.

hook.text: In 1849, Hippolyte Fizeau measured the speed of light using a spinning toothed wheel and a mirror.

buildup.text: He aimed light through a gap in the wheel at a mirror 8 km away. It bounced back through the next gap.

discovery.text: By adjusting the wheel's speed, he calculated light at 315,000 km/s—only 5% off the real value.

twist.text: Léon Foucault improved the method using rotating mirrors and got within 0.6% of the correct answer.

climax.text: Albert Michelson refined it further, winning the 1907 Nobel Prize for precision optical measurements.

punchline.text: A spinning wheel and a mirror. Close enough to measure the universe.

quiz.question: How did Fizeau measure the speed of light?
options: Using a spinning toothed wheel and a distant mirror
